description: Kyoto-based niche manufacturer of plasma process equipment for compound semiconductors and advanced devices. Product lines: (1) Deposition — PECVD, ALD/PEALD, LSCVD, DLC coating; (2) Etching — ICP-RIE, DRIE (Deep Silicon), CCP-RIE, XeF2, ALE (Atomic Layer Etching), Cryo-ICP; (3) Surface Treatment — Aqua Plasma cleaners, UV-Ozone cleaners. Key applications: GaN/SiC power devices, GaN RF (5G/6G), GaAs/InP VCSELs and laser diodes, BAW/SAW filters, MEMS, MicroLEDs, TSV/advanced packaging, quantum computing research. Over 5,000 systems deployed in 36 countries. Asset-light fablight model: all design, R&D, software, testing and sales in-house; manufacturing fully outsourced to domestic Japanese subcontractors. Overseas revenue ~38–40% of total with mid-term target of >50%. Holds equity stakes in ADTEC Plasma Technology (RF power supply supplier) and Murata Manufacturing (component supplier and equipment customer). Company name is acronym: Semiconductor And Materials COmpany. Founded 1979–1980 by Osamu Tsuji; Japan's first plasma CVD equipment (1980). TSE Prime Market.

majorSuppliers: ADTEC Plasma Technology Co., Ltd. (CMP-0114) — RF generators and plasma power supplies; SAMCO holds minor equity stake confirming strategic supply relationship
Murata Manufacturing Co., Ltd. (6981.T, CMP-0113) — electronic components used in SAMCO equipment; SAMCO holds minor equity stake
Ferrotec Holdings Corporation (6890.T, CMP-0009) — quartz components, ceramics parts for process chambers
Horiba Ltd. / Horiba STEC (6856.T, CMP-0012) — mass flow controllers (MFCs) for gas delivery systems in CVD/etch tools
Ebara Corporation (6361.T, CMP-0036) — dry vacuum pumps integrated into process equipment
Edwards Vacuum (Atlas Copco) — vacuum pumps and turbo pumps
MKS Instruments — pressure gauges, process control instruments
Domestic precision machining subcontractors (Kyoto/Osaka region) — stainless steel and aluminum process chambers, custom machined parts
Note: securities report lists some parts with limited/sole-source suppliers; SAMCO actively pursuing dual-sourcing to reduce risk
majorClients: Skyworks Filter Solutions Japan Co., Ltd. — BAW/SAW filter manufacturing in Osaka (explicitly named in SAMCO securities filing as production customer)
Nichia Corporation (CMP-0112) — LED and laser diode manufacturing
Sony Semiconductor Manufacturing Co., Ltd. — image sensors and compound semiconductor devices
Kanazawa Murata Manufacturing Co., Ltd. (subsidiary of Murata Mfg, CMP-0113) — BAW filter manufacturing
Rohm Co., Ltd. (6963.T, CMP-0111) — SiC power devices and GaN RF devices
RIKEN Center for Advanced Photonics — R&D systems
NICT (National Institute of Information and Communications Technology) — R&D systems
Japanese universities: Kyushu Univ., Nagoya Univ., Osaka Metropolitan Univ., Univ. of Tokyo, Hokkaido Univ., Kyoto Univ.
US universities: Penn State, Northwestern (NUFAB), Yale, MIT
European partner: OST – Eastern Switzerland University of Applied Sciences
Historical early production customers: Motorola (US), Arco Solar (US), Hitachi, Toshiba, Fujitsu

etching: DRIE (Deep Reactive Ion Etching, Bosch process)

keyApplications: SiC (Silicon Carbide) power devices — EV inverters, industrial power
GaN power devices — fast chargers, EV onboard converters
GaN RF devices — 5G/6G base stations, satellite
GaAs/InP VCSELs — 3D sensing (Face ID, LiDAR), data center interconnects
TSV (Through-Silicon Via) / Advanced Packaging — chiplet integration
Quantum computing — research-grade qubit fabrication tools

shareholderStructure.foreignOwnership: ~5–10% (institutional foreign holders ~1.19% per SEC 13F filers; IR meetings show 17/103 overseas investors Aug 2024–Jul 2025)

financialHighlights.businessModel: Fablight: outsources manufacturing; retains R&D, software, inspection, and direct sales in-house
financialHighlights.midTermStrategy: Raise overseas sales ratio to >50%; deepen penetration in SiC/GaN power devices and 5G filter markets; third R&D facility planned
equityInvestments: ADTEC Plasma Technology — minor stake; RF plasma power supply supplier
Murata Manufacturing (6981.T) — minor stake; component supplier and equipment customer

competitors: Oxford Instruments Plasma Technology (UK) — ICP-RIE and PECVD for compound semiconductors
Plasma-Therm LLC (US) — ICP-RIE, PECVD, ALD systems
SPTS Technologies (UK, part of Orbotech/KLA) — etch and deposition for compound semiconductors and MEMS
ULVAC Inc. (6728.T) — broad CVD, PVD, and dry etch equipment
Sumitomo Heavy Industries Advanced Machinery (SHI-AT, 6302.T) — plasma equipment
Lam Research (LRCX) — large-scale production etch and CVD (different scale/market focus)
keyRisks: Niche market concentration: compound semiconductor is smaller and more cyclical than silicon mainstream
Fablight dependency: quality and delivery depend on domestic manufacturing subcontractors
Sole-source supplier risk: some specialized parts from limited suppliers; dual-sourcing program in progress
FX sensitivity: ~38–40% overseas revenue; risk increases as overseas share grows toward >50% target
R&D customer base: universities generate low-volume, high-customization orders with limited recurring revenue
Competitive pressure from larger equipment makers (ULVAC, Lam Research, Oxford Instruments) in adjacent markets
Founder/family share concentration: ~44% held by Tsuji family/entities; limits governance and liquidity
growthDrivers: SiC power device boom: EV adoption driving massive SiC wafer fab buildout globally (Rohm, STMicro, Wolfspeed, Infineon)
GaN RF: 5G/6G base station and satellite infrastructure demand for GaN-on-SiC and GaN-on-Si devices
BAW/SAW filters: Smartphone 5G requires more filters per device; Murata, Qorvo, Skyworks all expanding production
Compound semiconductor renaissance: III-V materials for photonics, quantum computing, power electronics
MicroLED: Display technology shift requires mass transfer and III-V LED production at scale
Geographic expansion: Growing from Japan-centric to global; EU, US, and SEA market development
_externalRelationships.notes: SAMCO is Japan's pioneer in plasma CVD equipment (established 1980, Japan's first system). International website: https://www.samcointl.com. Stock 1-year return: +104% (¥3,100 → ¥6,310). Market cap doubled from ~¥33.7B (Wave 3 capture) to ~¥50.7B. High founder ownership and low foreign ownership suggest undiscovered/underfollowed stock. Growing strategically in power semiconductors (SiC/GaN) and 5G/6G filter manufacturing equipment.
